steps.title: Identify the context clue
steps.content: The sentence describes a clerk showing Mr. Adams a vase "marked at about twelve or fourteen dollars." The phrase "at about twelve or fourteen dollars" immediately follows "marked" and specifies a dollar amount, signaling that the word describes how a retail price is displayed on merchandise.

steps.title: Test each choice in context
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Staged': "Staged at twelve or fourteen dollars" has no meaning in a retail context. "Staged" refers to arranging or setting a scene, not labeling a price. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Priced': "Priced at about twelve or fourteen dollars" is the standard way to say that a price tag on merchandise shows a particular amount. This fits the retail context exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Stained': "Stained at twelve or fourteen dollars" is nonsensical; "stained" refers to discoloration, not a monetary value. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Watched': "Watched at twelve or fourteen dollars" is equally nonsensical; "watched" has no connection to pricing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
